ew_context: NZDJPY completing textbook inverse H&S pattern at cycle low - likely major wave 2 or B wave conclusion before next impulsive advance
pattern_desc: Classic inverse head and shoulders with head at 90.69 and neckline resistance at 94.24. Pattern spans multiple weeks with clear volume confirmation on right shoulder formation.
key_levels: Neckline: 94.24 (pattern trigger). Head: 90.69 (pattern low). Resistance: 97.80 (measured move target)

market_context: JPY weakness theme intact while NZD benefits from hawkish RBNZ stance and commodity strength. Risk-on flows supporting carry trades
full_analysis:
## Market Structure & Technical Context

NZDJPY has formed a textbook inverse head and shoulders pattern with the head established at 90.69 and neckline resistance clearly defined at 94.24. The pattern has developed over several weeks with the right shoulder at 92.14 showing strong rejection of lower levels. Current price at 93.80 is positioned just below the neckline trigger, suggesting imminent breakout potential. This pattern represents completion of a major corrective cycle, likely ending wave 2 or B wave territory before the next impulsive advance.

RSI at 66.7% shows building momentum with room for further upside, while EMA alignment is constructive with EMA20 at 93.22 above EMA50 at 92.76. ATR of 0.72 indicates normal volatility supportive of pattern completion. CFD physics show Reynolds 0.73 with laminar flow and momentum alignment, confirming the bullish technical setup.

## Key Levels

- **Resistance**: 94.24 (neckline breakout trigger), 97.80 (measured move target)
- **Support**: 92.14 (right shoulder low), 90.69 (head/pattern low)

## Invalidation Level

Close below 92.00 on D1 timeframe would break the right shoulder structure and negate the inverse head and shoulders pattern.

## Macro & Flow Context

JPY weakness theme remains intact amid BoJ dovish policy while NZD benefits from RBNZ hawkish stance and commodity price strength. Risk-on sentiment supporting higher-yielding carry trade positions.
